text:
*[Bug 19008] on.exit() at top level is a silent no-op*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19008>

--- Comment #2 from Kevin Ushey (<mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>) ---
Thinking about this a bit more, execution of top-level `on.exit()` invocations
on exit would almost certainly be undesired in interactive sessions -- one
might interactively run the same `on.exit()` calls multiple times in an
interactive session while e.g. debugging code / stepping through tests, and the
actual time at which the user would intend those `on.exit()` expressions to be
run isn't very well defined. Relatedly, any sort of message / warning output
would likely be annoying in this scenario.

For non-interactive sessions, it might make more sense to run top-level
handlers on process exit at some appropriate time, but it's hard for me to
argue it's worth the effort when the user could just do something like `main <-
function(...) {}; main()`, and put their exit handlers in the `main` function.

To wit, I think I agree on the balance it's best to keep the code as-is. I
still think it's worth documenting this explicitly though. Some proposals:

```
--- src/library/base/man/on.exit.Rd
+++ src/library/base/man/on.exit.Rd
@@ -41,6 +41,8 @@ on.exit(expr = NULL, add = FALSE, after = TRUE)
   If multiple \code{on.exit} expressions are set using \code{add = TRUE}
   then all expressions will be run even if one signals an error.

+  Top-level \code{on.exit} invocations are silently discarded.
+
   This is a \sQuote{special} \link{primitive} function: it only
   evaluates the arguments \code{add} and \code{after}.
```

Or

```
--- src/library/base/man/on.exit.Rd
+++ src/library/base/man/on.exit.Rd
@@ -41,6 +41,9 @@ on.exit(expr = NULL, add = FALSE, after = TRUE)
   If multiple \code{on.exit} expressions are set using \code{add = TRUE}
   then all expressions will be run even if one signals an error.

+  Top-level \code{on.exit} invocations are silently discarded, as there
+  is no evaluation frame with which to associate the provided \code{expr}.
+
   This is a \sQuote{special} \link{primitive} function: it only
   evaluates the arguments \code{add} and \code{after}.
```

since IMHO this fact is not immediately obvious to non-experts.

text:
*[Bug 18972] diff(x=<m-by-n>, l, d) drops dimensions when l*d >= m*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18972>

--- Comment #11 from Suharto Anggono (<mailto:suharto_anggono@yahoo.com|suharto_anggono@yahoo.com>) ---
(In reply to Suharto Anggono from comment #10)
    r[-nrow(r):-max(nrow(r) - lag + 1L, 0L), , drop = FALSE]

and

    r[-length(r):-max(length(r) - lag + 1L, 0L)]

are also correct and avoid sequence direction reversal.

elements.text: *[Bug 18972] diff(x=<m-by-n>, l, d) drops dimensions when l*d >= m*
